principle: Apply Material Design 3 color roles not hardcoded colors — the role system (primary, on-primary, container, on-container) guarantees accessible contrast across all generated themes
explanation: X.mobile|m3_color_roles: hardcoded_hex -> breaks_when_theme_changes; M3_roles: primary(brand_CTA)+on-primary(text_on_primary)+primary-container(muted_bg)+on-primary-container(text_on_container)+secondary+tertiary+error+surface+on-surface; dynamic_color(Android12+): seed_color -> full_palette_generated_by_system; minimum_contrast: 4.5:1_guaranteed_by_role_system; NO:hardcoded_colors NO:swapping_primary+on-primary_roles; eg: Button(background=Color(0xFF6200EE)) >> Button(containerColor=MaterialTheme.colorScheme.primary)

principle: Use Material Design 3 typography scale consistently — the 15-style scale (Display/Headline/Title/Body/Label in three sizes each) maps semantic content roles to specific type treatments
explanation: X.mobile|m3_type_scale: ad_hoc_text_sizes -> inconsistent_hierarchy; M3_scale: displayLarge/Medium/Small(57/45/36sp) for_hero_text; headlineLarge/Medium/Small(32/28/24sp) for_sections; titleLarge/Medium/Small(22/16/14sp) for_cards+appbars; bodyLarge/Medium/Small(16/14/12sp) for_content; labelLarge/Medium/Small(14/12/11sp) for_buttons+captions; Compose: MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge; NO:arbitrary_sp_sizes NO:using_display_for_body_text; eg: Text(fontSize=18.sp) >> Text(style=MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge)

principle: Choose navigation component based on destination count — bottom bar for 3-5 destinations, navigation rail for tablet landscape, navigation drawer for 6+ destinations
explanation: X.mobile|m3_navigation: destination_count_determines_pattern; bottom_navigation_bar: 3-5_destinations+phone; navigation_rail: 3-7_destinations+tablet/landscape; navigation_drawer: 5+_destinations+tablet/desktop_or_collapsed_on_phone; modal_drawer: phone_with_many_destinations(behind_hamburger); NavigationBar_vs_DrawerSheet: NOT_mixed_on_same_screen; NO:hamburger_on_phone_with_3_destinations NO:bottom_bar_with_7_destinations; eg: 4_destinations_with_hamburger >> NavigationBar_with_4_items

principle: Use elevation via surface tint not drop shadows in Material Design 3 — M3 communicates elevation by blending primary color into the surface, not by casting shadows
explanation: X.mobile|m3_elevation: M2_shadows: box_shadows_for_depth; M3_elevation: surface_tint_color(primary_overlaid_at_opacity) + optional_shadow; tint_levels: tone1(5%_primary)+tone2(8%)+tone3(11%)+tone4(12%)+tone5(14%); effect: elevated_cards_appear_slightly_tinted_not_shadowed; tonalSurface: Card+Sheet+Dialog default; NO:adding_heavy_box_shadows_to_M3_components NO:using_M2_elevation_system_in_M3_app; eg: Card(elevation=8dp_shadow) >> Card(tonalElevation=4.dp) using_surface_tint

principle: Use FAB for the single most important action on a screen — a Floating Action Button represents one promoted action, never a menu or a secondary action
explanation: X.mobile|m3_fab: FAB_purpose: one_promoted_primary_action_per_screen; types: FAB(56dp)+SmallFAB(40dp)+LargeFAB(96dp)+ExtendedFAB(icon+label); extended_FAB: preferred_when_action_needs_label; collapse_extended_FAB_on_scroll_down+expand_on_scroll_up; placement: bottom_right_in_thumb_zone; NO:FAB_for_navigation NO:FAB_for_secondary_actions NO:two_FABs_on_same_screen; eg: FAB_with_share+edit+delete_as_speed_dial >> FAB_for_compose_only+others_in_appbar+context_menu

principle: Apply M3 shape scale to communicate component personality — smaller radius reads as professional, larger radius reads as expressive and friendly
explanation: X.mobile|m3_shape_scale: shape_tokens: none(0dp)+extraSmall(4dp)+small(8dp)+medium(12dp)+large(16dp)+extraLarge(28dp)+full(50%); component_defaults: TextButton=full+Card=medium+Dialog=extraLarge+BottomSheet=extraLarge+Chip=small; customizing: override_MaterialTheme.shapes not hardcode_per_component; brand_feel: 0dp=strict/technical, full_radius=playful; NO:hardcoded_cornerRadius_in_each_composable; eg: RoundedCornerShape(8.dp)_per_composable >> MaterialTheme.shapes.small_applied_globally

principle: Use state layers to communicate interaction states — M3 overlays a semi-transparent layer of the content color at defined opacities for hover, pressed, focused, and dragged states
explanation: X.mobile|m3_state_layers: interaction_feedback: must_be_visible; state_layer_opacities: hover=8%+focus=12%+pressed=12%+dragged=16% of_content_color_over_surface; implementation: Indication API in Compose handles_automatically; custom_components: use_LocalIndication or Modifier.indication; ripple: disabled_in_M3_by_default -> StateLayer_instead; NO:custom_color_changes_for_hover_without_M3_system NO:missing_focus_state_on_interactive_elements; eg: custom_darken_on_click >> Modifier.clickable() with_default_M3_state_layer

principle: Use Snackbar for brief non-blocking feedback and dialogs only for decisions requiring acknowledgment — most app feedback should not block the user's current flow
explanation: X.mobile|m3_feedback_patterns: dialog: use_only_for_irreversible_or_required_decisions; Snackbar: transient_feedback(3-4s)+optional_action(Undo); Toast_equivalent: Snackbar_without_action; banners: persistent_messages_requiring_action; inline_errors: for_form_validation(NO_dialogs_for_validation); NO:dialog_for_"operation_successful" NO:blocking_snackbar; eg: AlertDialog("Item_deleted","OK") >> Snackbar("Item_deleted",action="Undo")

principle: Support predictive back gesture on Android 13+ — the predictive back animation previews the destination before the user commits to going back
explanation: X.mobile|android_predictive_back: Android13+(API33): system_back_preview_animation; opt_in: android:enableOnBackInvokedCallback=true in manifest; implement: OnBackPressedCallback or Compose BackHandler; custom_back_handler: intercept_only_when_necessary + call_proceed() otherwise; wrong: always_intercepting_back -> disables_predictive_animation; NO:overriding_system_back_for_whole_app NO:OnBackPressed_deprecated_without_replacement; eg: @Override_onBackPressed() >> OnBackPressedDispatcher_with_callback

principle: Use adaptive layouts with WindowSizeClass to serve phone, tablet, and foldable from one codebase — hardcoded phone layouts break on tablets and foldables
explanation: X.mobile|adaptive_layout: single_layout_hardcoded -> broken_on_tablet+foldable; WindowSizeClass: Compact(phone_portrait)+Medium(tablet_portrait/foldable)+Expanded(tablet_landscape); pattern: Compact=single_pane+BottomNav; Medium=single_pane+NavigationRail; Expanded=two_pane+NavigationRail/Drawer; Compose: calculateWindowSizeClass()+when(sizeClass.widthSizeClass); NO:hardcoded_dp_widths_for_layout NO:phone_only_testing; eg: single_column_layout_hardcoded >> adaptive_WindowSizeClass_with_two-pane_on_Expanded
